title: ce-verify-work per-unit verdict
description: Structured output contract for one Implementation Unit's classification. Classifier agents (and the prose fallback) emit one of these per unit; the deterministic roll-up consumes them. The verdict enum is the single source of truth shared with drift-rollup.js (a consistency test guards the two against drift).

u_id.description: The unit's U-ID, verbatim from the plan heading (e.g. 'U3'). Never renumbered.

verdict.description: done = Files present and Verification satisfied by current repo state. remaining = no git evidence the unit was attempted (no commit touched its declared paths) — progress, not rework. drifted = a commit touched the unit's declared Files but the repo diverged (Verification unmet, Files partial/deleted) — rework-shaped. unverifiable = the Verification is intrinsically behavioral/runtime and cannot be settled from static repo state. A statically-checkable unit is NEVER unverifiable.

evidence.description: Cited artifacts anchoring the verdict: commit SHAs, repo-relative file paths, diff-hunk references. Non-empty and REQUIRED for done and drifted (drifted cites both the attempt evidence and the divergence). Optional for remaining and unverifiable.

rationale.description: One sentence on why this verdict holds. For unverifiable, name the behavioral/runtime criterion that blocks static settlement.
